data.title: Nvidia CEO Huang says firm's $30B investment in OpenAI likely a prelude to IPO
data.description:
• Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang said on Wednesday that his company's recent $30B investment into OpenAI (OPENAI) “might be the last time” it invests in the ChatGPT maker, as it is likely to go public. 
  • Speaking at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference, Huang said the proposed $100B investment into OpenAI, which the two companies mentioned in September, was likely “not in the cards.” 
  • “The reason for that is because they’re going to go public,” Huang said, according to comments obtained by CNBC. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is set to appear at the conference on Thursday. 
  • Huang also noted that Nvidia's $10B investment into Anthropic (ANTHRO) would also likely be its last.
  • Nvidia shares rose 2.4% in midday trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Ross Stores' ambitious targets are achievable given standout quarter -- analysts
data.description:
Driven by higher comparable store sales, a refreshed merchandise selection, and limited price hikes despite tariff pressures—especially in home furnishings—Ross Stores (ROST) delivered an impressive quarter, beating on both the top- and bottom-line and setting guidance that appears conservative despite forecasts for 7%-8% Q1 comparable store sales.  

Reiterated as one of his top picks, Jefferies’ Corey Tarlowe called it “a standout Q4,” with broad-based strength, improving merchandising margins, and disciplined inventory.

Forecasts for 7% to 8% comparable sales growth in the current quarter surprised most analysts given Ross Stores’ tendency to set conservative guidance. But the lofty goal is surprisingly achievable thanks to improvements to marketing and the in-store experience, as well as improvements in the merchandise selection.

Additionally, while many other retailers are bracing for tighter consumer spending—especially in an environment of higher fuel prices—Ross Stores (ROST) “has a long history of being able to grow in good and bad macroeconomic environments,” BofA Securities’ Lorraine Hutchinson said in her note to clients.

To achieve the ambitious sales goals the company set for itself, there are several levers at its disposal that will generate increased traffic and drive double-digit revenue growth. Evercore ISI’s Michael Binetti points to Ross Stores’ (ROST) ongoing marketing strategy, improving in-store efficiency (with self-checkout), more inventory, and more productive new stores, all of which will enable the company to meet or exceed guidance.

While the future looks bright, investors should be mindful that Ross Stores (ROST) has been investing heavily in new distribution centers with high operational costs that could pressure margins. 

Additionally, as Seeking Alpha analyst Dividend Yield Theorist also points out, Ross (ROST) buys inventory “opportunistically,” which means it is vulnerable to inconsistencies “when costs become inventory and when revenue hits the financials, creating a possible lumpiness effect on earnings.”

However, Ross Stores (ROST) has so far proven adept at managing these headwinds—including tariffs—more effectively than peers. Its 53% year-over-year share price gain, compared with 31% for TJX Companies (TJX) and 16% for the S&P 500, suggests investors remain confident in its ability to weather further challenges.

“With improving merchandise margins, easing tariff pressure, and accelerating store growth, we believe ROST remains well positioned to gain share and drive steady EBIT expansion over time,” Tarlowe adds.

Buoyed by Tuesday's results, guidance, and a dividend hike, shares of Ross Stores (ROST) set another all-time high on Wednesday.

data.title: Hyperscalers have reached ‘peak negativity’ about capex – analyst
data.description:
The major hyperscalers have reached “peak negativity” when it comes to investor concerns about their massive spending, according to Barton Crockett, senior analyst at Rosenblatt Securities.

In an interview with CNBC, Crockett argued that while capital expenditure at Amazon (AMZN), Google/Alphabet (GOOGL), (GOOG), Meta (META), and Microsoft (MSFT) has surged dramatically, the narrative is poised to improve as spending levels stabilize.

The numbers behind the concern are stark: aggregate capex for the four major hyperscalers has grown approximately 66% year-over-year, a surge that Crockett says is “basically evaporating free cash flow at those companies.”

However, he believes the worst of the anxiety may be behind investors. “I think you’re at peak concern about the spending, peak concern about their ability to convert that into profits,” Crockett said.

In contrast to the hyperscalers, Apple (AAPL) stands out for its relative stability. The company is “not plowing capex into huge data centers like the hyperscalers,” allowing it to navigate current market turbulence more effectively.

Crockett described Apple’s (AAPL) ability to remain steady despite memory cost surges and supply chain issues as “a flex” that speaks to the company’s power, adding that recent announcements are consistent with meaningful gross margin improvement in the coming months.

Among the hyperscalers, Crockett identified Meta (META) as a top recommendation due to strong revenue growth and expected returns on investment.

He also pointed to Amazon (AMZN) as an interesting opportunity if the company can manage “the noise with Anthropic (ANTHRO).”

The analyst suggested that the current hardware-focused cycle could give way to gains in services and software stocks.

On Netflix (NFLX), Crockett maintains a hold rating, calling it “a well-managed company in a maturing environment.” He noted that while the company’s EBITDA is growing at about 20%, challenges loom as younger viewers shift toward user-generated content platforms where Netflix is less competitive. Viewership growth, he said, is no longer “rocket ship” territory.

Looking ahead, Crockett predicts a shift in the tech narrative as hyperscalers begin to “milk the revenue from the investments that they’ve made.”

He expressed confidence that capex growth will not repeat the two-thirds surge next year, creating a potentially more favorable setup for these companies as they transition from heavy spending to revenue realization.

data.title: AI and chip stocks recover, while surge in oil prices eases amid US-Iran war
data.description:
Chip and artificial intelligence-related stocks saw some relief on Wednesday as oil prices eased for the first time since the U.S.-Iran war escalations began.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) rose around 1.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) climbed about 0.8%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) grew nearly 0.6%.

The U.S. Senate is set to vote Wednesday on President Donald Trump’s decision to launch military action against Iran, marking a major congressional test as the conflict rapidly expands across the Middle East without a clearly defined U.S. exit strategy. Over the weekend, the U.S. and Israel carried out coordinated attacks on Iran that killed senior military and government leadership, including Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, triggering an Iranian response.

Chip stocks had tumbled on Tuesday after Asian markets saw red amid potential impact on energy supplies and surging oil prices due to the ongoing clashes. However, on Wednesday the surge in oil prices eased, and U.S. markets opened more positively after a better-than-expected labor report and a lower U.S. Composite PMI read.

AI chipmaker Nvidia's (NVDA) stock rose about 2% on Wednesday. Broadcom (AVGO) rose roughly 2%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) was largely flat but in the green.

Several other AI and networking-related stocks were also among the gainers. Arista Networks (ANET) surged about 7%, while Ciena (CIEN) and Celestica (CLS) each jumped roughly 3%. Cisco (CSCO) rose around 1%. However, Coherent (COHR) and Lumentum (LITE) seem to buck the trend, declining about 5% and 6%, respectively.

Shares of Intel (INTC) soared about 6% on Wednesday after CFO David Zinsner's comments at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference. Zinsner said that the server CPU market is expected to grow meaningfully in 2026 and that demand for servers is strong across the board. However, Zinsner noted that the supply is a greater challenge into next year. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), a rival to Intel in the server CPU market, jumped nearly 4%.

Separately, on Wednesday, a bipartisan group of U.S. senators raised national security concerns about Intel, the New York Times reported. The lawmakers expressed concern about Intel's use of a tool from a subsidiary of China-based ACM Research, which is blacklisted in the U.S., and whether China could gain access to Intel’s chip-making processes.

An Intel spokesperson told the news outlet that “all Intel activities fully comply with applicable U.S. laws and regulations, and we engage regularly with the U.S. government on security matters.” The spokesperson added that Intel takes national security responsibilities “extremely seriously” and has systems set up to “limit the information any individual tool can receive during the manufacturing process.”

Micron Technology (MU) soared around 7%, while Arm (ARM) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) each climbed about 2%. Marvell Technology (MRVL), Analog Devices (ADI), and Texas Instruments (TXN) each rose nearly 1%. However, Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) fell about 2%, and GlobalFoundries (GFS) dipped roughly 1%.

Chip equipment makers: Lam Research (LRCX) soared about 4%, while ASML (ASML), Applied Materials (AMAT), and KLA (KLAC) each rose about 3%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

(This story has been updated to include additional information about Intel.)

data.title: More Fed districts report flat to declining economic activity in latest Fed's Beige Book report
data.description:
U.S. economic activity increased at a "slight to moderate" pace in seven of the Federal Reserve's 12 districts, with the remaining five observing flat or declining activity, according to the Federal Reserve's Beige Book report released on Wednesday. That compared with four districts reporting flat or declining activity in the prior report issued in mid-January.

On balance, consumer spending rose slightly, although two districts noted ongoing declines. "Many (districts) noted that sales were dampened by economic uncertainty, increased price sensitivity, and lower-income consumers pulling back on spending," the report said.

Districts impacted by winter storms generally saw slower retail traffic, while one district said immigration enforcement activity hurt customer demand in urban areas.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Justice Department investigates fertilizer producers over possible price collusion
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice is investigating several of the largest fertilizer manufacturers over concerns they may have coordinated pricing, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter. The report triggered a sudden intraday decline in shares of fertilizer makers.

Companies under scrutiny include Nutrien (NTR), Mosaic Company (MOS), CF Industries (CF), Koch Industries and Yara International (YARIY) (YRAIF). Together these firms control much of the nitrogen fertilizer sold in the United States.

The inquiry, being handled by the Justice Department’s antitrust division in Chicago, is examining whether the companies’ pricing practices could violate antitrust laws. The probe is still in early stages and authorities have not accused the companies of wrongdoing.

Fertilizer markets in the U.S. are highly concentrated, a situation that has drawn scrutiny from farmers and policymakers as production costs remain elevated. Prices surged after geopolitical disruptions including the war in Ukraine and have stayed relatively high despite some easing in recent months.

Industry watchdog Farm Action estimates that Nutrien (NTR) and Mosaic (MOS) control about 90% of U.S. potash and phosphate production capacity. Nutrien (NTR), CF Industries (CF), Koch and Yara (YARIY) (YRAIF) together account for roughly 82% of nitrogen fertilizer supply.

High input costs have been a growing concern for farmers already facing weaker crop prices and tighter margins. Rising tensions in the Middle East have recently added new uncertainty to global fertilizer supply chains, pushing up prices for products such as urea.

The investigation reflects broader efforts by federal officials to examine competition in agricultural markets. The Justice Department has also launched probes into pricing among major egg producers and other segments of the U.S. food supply chain.

data.title: Valero climbs for a seventh session
data.description:
Shares of Valero Energy (VLO) rose 3.03% to $224.30 in the afternoon trade on Wednesday, adding to six straight sessions of gains.

The stock has gained around 9.22% between February 24 and March 3 compared to a 1.07% fall of the S&P 500 during the same period.

The rise comes amid energy markets reacting to geopolitical and sector-specific developments. Oil prices steadied near recent highs after widening U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran disrupted flows through the Strait of Hormuz, stoking concerns over prolonged supply constraints, while headlines pointed to gas and oil prices soaring amid fears the conflict could drag on. 

Investors also monitored regulatory developments after the EPA was reported to shift at least half of waived biofuel obligations to big refiners, even as Valero was removed from Goldman Sachs’ U.S. conviction list earlier in the period.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, VLO is rated a Buy, with a score of 4.21 out of 5, receiving an A- for profitability, B+ in momentum, but scoring a D in terms of valuation.

An Seeking Alpha analysis pointed out that Valero shares are at an all-time high with valuation “1.5–2x above the company's historical average” despite profit margins that are “slightly below the 25-year average,” arguing the current pricing does not align with underlying profitability.

The analyst added that gasoline inventories have surged “from a five-year low to a five-year high,” warning this could weigh on the summer driving season, but maintained a Hold rating, pointing out VLO “has consistently been the low-cost provider among any operator,” ensuring that “no matter how bad the cycle gets, VLO will be in the best shape.”

Turning to the Wall Street, 11 of 19 analysts rate the stock Buy or higher, seven recommend Hold, and one recommends Sell.

Shares have gained around 14% in the past one month, and is up approximately 33.25% year-to-date.

data.title: Dycom projects up to $7.15B revenue for FY 2027 as Power Solutions acquisition drives diversification
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dycom Industries, Inc. (DY) Q4 2026

Management View

  • CEO Daniel Peyovich stated that "Dycom's fourth quarter results are an excellent finish to a record year as we set new benchmarks across nearly every financial metric we track." He highlighted that the company "exceeded the high end of our annual revenue outlook" and delivered record fourth quarter revenue of $1.46 billion, with organic revenue up 16.6% compared to the prior year. He emphasized the strategic acquisition of Power Solutions, noting it positions Dycom "at the intersection of digital infrastructure and the burgeoning data center market." Peyovich also pointed to a record $9.5 billion backlog, with $6.3 billion expected to be completed over the next 12 months, and a book-to-bill ratio of 1.3x.

  • Peyovich detailed that adjusted EBITDA reached $162.4 million for the quarter and non-GAAP adjusted diluted EPS was $2.03, a 42% increase year-over-year. He said, "Our approach to the pipeline remains disciplined. We are optimizing for high-value engagement that balances risk with superior returns."

  • CFO H. DeFerrari stated, "We delivered record annual results in fiscal 2026 with strong revenue growth, significant margin expansion and robust free cash flow." He reported that organic revenue grew 16.6% in Q4, with $95.8 million in acquired revenues from Power Solutions. DeFerrari added, "Consolidated adjusted EBITDA of $162.4 million increased 39.6% over Q4 '25."

Outlook

  • Dycom projects FY 2027 total revenue to be between $6.85 billion and $7.15 billion, representing growth of approximately 23.6% to 29% year-over-year, or 6.6% to 10.3% on an organic basis. CFO DeFerrari indicated, "For the Communications segment, we expect contract revenues to range from $5.70 billion to $5.90 billion... For the Building Systems segment, we expect contract revenues ranging from $1.15 billion to $1.25 billion."

  • Management outlined expectations for continued adjusted EBITDA margin expansion, with modest gains in Communications and mid-teens margins for Building Systems. Power Solutions is expected to drive 15% to 25% growth in the Building Systems segment.

  • For Q1 FY 2027, Dycom expects total contract revenues of $1.64 billion to $1.71 billion, adjusted EBITDA of $202 million to $218 million, and adjusted diluted EPS of $2.57 to $2.90.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue reached $1.46 billion, a 34.4% increase over Q4 FY 2025, with organic revenue up 16.6%. Adjusted EBITDA margin improved by 41 basis points to 11.1%. Non-GAAP adjusted diluted EPS was $2.03. Operating cash flow increased 27.7% to $419 million for the quarter, and DSOs improved by 13 days year-over-year to 101 days.

  • Full year revenue was $5.55 billion, up 17.9% from the prior year, with organic revenue up 6.5%. Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was $737.7 million with a margin of 13.3%. Free cash flow more than doubled to $435.3 million.

  • At quarter end, Dycom reported cash and equivalents of $709.2 million and total liquidity of $1.46 billion. Pro forma net leverage was 2.3x adjusted EBITDA, with a clear path to reduce to 2x over the next 12 months.

Q&A

  • Sangita Jain, KeyBanc Capital Markets Inc.: Asked about expansion plans for Power Solutions and cross-selling opportunities. CEO Peyovich responded that integration is proceeding well, highlighting early cross-sell success, particularly with hyperscalers, and outlined a 15% to 25% growth target for Power Solutions.

  • Eric Luebchow, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC: Inquired about the long-haul, middle mile, and inside defense market, previously estimated at $20 billion. CEO Peyovich stated, "We are getting more phone calls and seeing more opportunities than we saw even a quarter ago or frankly, even a week ago, the demand is that strong."

  • Frank Louthan, Raymond James & Associates: Asked about Power Solutions' growth rate and exposure to Echostar. Peyovich confirmed no exposure to Echostar and reaffirmed Power Solutions' 4-year CAGR of 15%, now targeting 15% to 25% for the coming year.

  • Michael Dudas, Vertical Research Partners: Questioned margin investment pace versus FY 2026. Peyovich explained margin growth will continue but at a slower pace than the prior year due to ongoing growth investments and labor additions.

  • Judah Aronovitz, UBS: Asked about Building Systems margin potential and SG&A. Peyovich described mid-teens as the right margin profile currently, with DeFerrari noting $18 million in transaction costs in SG&A for the quarter.

  • Richard Choe, JPMorgan: Requested clarification on hyperscale opportunity and acquisitions’ geographic focus. Peyovich said the company is not limited to DMV area and considers expansion into new, high-growth markets.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on growth rates, margin sustainability, and integration execution, reflecting a slightly positive but cautious tone, particularly regarding the scalability of Power Solutions and BEAD program timeline.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently highlighting disciplined growth, operational execution, and robust demand drivers. Peyovich repeatedly emphasized, "We feel really good about the growth profile" and pointed to strong customer response for cross-selling opportunities. There was some caution when discussing margin expansion pacing and labor investments.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone shifted from excitement about the acquisition to confidence in executing integration and scaling the new segment, with ongoing optimism about secular demand drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY 2027 revenue and margin expansion represents a higher growth trajectory than the prior quarter’s outlook, now including the impact of Power Solutions.

  • Strategic focus has shifted from acquisition announcement and integration planning (Q3) to operational execution, cross-sell, and margin discipline in Q4.

  • Analysts in the current quarter focused more heavily on Power Solutions integration, cross-selling synergies, margin sustainability, and labor force scalability compared to prior quarter’s questions on acquisition rationale and addressable market size.

  • Management’s confidence remains high, with a stronger emphasis on integration progress and capitalizing on digital infrastructure demand.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified potential industry-wide skilled labor shortages as a challenge, emphasizing ongoing investments in workforce development and training facilities.

  • Weather-related disruptions impacted margins in Q4. Seasonality and timing of funding for BEAD and other large-scale programs remain factors affecting revenue ramp.

  • Analysts inquired about margin drag from integration costs and headcount growth, with management highlighting ongoing discipline and investment in capabilities ahead of anticipated demand.

Final Takeaway

Dycom delivered record financial performance in Q4 and FY 2026, driven by robust organic growth, strategic entry into the data center market, and the integration of Power Solutions. Management projects significant revenue growth for FY 2027, with a disciplined focus on margin expansion, cash flow, and workforce investment to support intensifying demand drivers in digital infrastructure. The company is positioned to leverage its expanded platform for growth in both communications and building systems, supported by a record backlog and strengthening customer relationships across carriers and hyperscalers.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UBS sees Accenture’s Ookla deal as timely bet on AI-driven network intelligence
data.description:
Accenture’s (ACN) planned acquisition of network analytics firm Ookla is a well timed move that could strengthen the consulting giant’s position in artificial intelligence and digital infrastructure, according to UBS analyst Kevin McVeigh.

He said the roughly $1.2 billion transaction reflects a strategic expansion of Accenture’s (ACN) data capabilities as enterprises invest heavily in AI driven systems.

“We look favorably on the timely transaction,” McVeigh wrote in a March 3 research note, pointing to the importance of network level data as artificial intelligence systems become more deeply embedded in digital infrastructure.

Ookla, which generated about $231 million in revenue in 2025, provides network intelligence and analytics tools including Speedtest and Downdetector. UBS estimates the acquisition price implies roughly 5.2 times sales.

Data advantage in AI era

McVeigh said Ookla’s products provide insights across networks, devices and applications that can help organizations manage increasingly complex digital systems.

That data could support a range of enterprise use cases, from fraud detection in banking to smart home analytics in utilities and customer traffic optimization in retail.

The analyst said integrating these capabilities into Accenture’s (ACN) consulting and technology services should strengthen its ability to help telecom operators, hyperscale cloud providers and large enterprises optimize Wi-Fi and 5G networks.

Larger deal but consistent with strategy

The acquisition stands out because it is larger than many of Accenture’s (ACN) typical transactions. The company has historically relied on smaller tuck in acquisitions rather than deals exceeding $1 billion.

Even so, McVeigh said the purchase aligns with Accenture’s (ACN) broader strategy of disciplined inorganic growth tied to cloud modernization, AI and industry specific digital solutions.

UBS expects acquisitions to contribute about 1.5% of Accenture’s (ACN) revenue growth in fiscal 2026, with the company planning roughly $3 billion in deal spending compared with about $1.5 billion the prior year.

AI fears may be overdone

McVeigh also framed the acquisition as a response to investor concerns that artificial intelligence could reduce demand for consulting services.

In his view, the market may be underestimating Accenture’s (ACN) ability to benefit from AI adoption rather than be disrupted by it.

data.title: Europe faces inflation shock twice as large as U.S., TS Lombard's Perkins says
data.description:
Europe is facing an inflation shock about double that of the U.S. amid the deepening war in Iran, TS Lombard economist Dario Perkins said on Wednesday.

European natural gas prices surged more than 40% Tuesday following a 40% jump on Monday, as the production halt at the world's largest liquefied natural gas export facility in Qatar sparks fears over global supply and risks to energy security in Europe and Asia.

"Right now, Europe is looking at an inflation shock roughly double that of the US ~ 2%pts," Perkins wrote in a social media post.

Europe is more exposed than the U.S. as it imports most of its natural gas and relies heavily on global LNG markets, while the U.S. produces far more of its own energy.

Perkins told Seeking Alpha that "Europe's shock is much larger because nat gas prices are up much more in Europe." Meanwhile, "UK gas prices are up 90% but they are capped until July, luckily."

Meanwhile, Euro Area inflation rose 1.9% Y/Y in February, up from January’s 16-month low of 1.7% and above market expectations of 1.7%, according to a preliminary estimate released this week. Core inflation, which excludes energy, food, alcohol, and tobacco, rose to 2.4%, rebounding from January’s more than four-year low of 2.2%.

Natural gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), and (FCG)

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL)

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).
